zh.Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England: 英國諾廷加姆爾沃可索普
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Sutton I於大約1286年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾沃可索普時，他的父親Richard de Sutton21歲，母親Isabella Patrick27歲。他於1302年與Margaret Somery在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾華索普結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1338年2月在英國武斯特斯爾杜德利杜德利 卡素享年53歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭韋斯特 米德藍茲杜德利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Sutton I于大约1286年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾沃可索普时，他的父亲Richard de Sutton21岁，母亲Isabella Patrick27岁。他于1302年与Margaret Somery在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾華索普结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1338年2月在英国武斯特斯爾杜德利杜德利 卡素享年53岁，埋葬在英国英格兰韋斯特 米德藍茲杜德利。

nameMeanings.name: Sutton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Sutton, named with Old English sūth ‘south, southern’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The English surname is also common in Ireland (Wexford, Kildare), where it has been established since the 13th century and Gaelicized as de Sutún.
Jewish (from the former Ottoman Empire, including Syria): unexplained.
English: topographic name from Middle English (bi) suthentune ‘(at the place to the) south of the village’.

variants.name: Setton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avi, Ronit, Yossef.  French Andree, Henri, Jacques, Maryse.
Jewish (from the Ottoman Empire): altered form of   Sutton  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John Sutton I was born about 1286, in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Richard de Sutton, was 21 and his mother, Isabella Patrick, was 27. He married Margaret Somery in 1302, in Warsop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died about February 1338, in Dudley Castle, Dudley, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 53, and was buried in Dudley, West Midlands, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Sutton I est né environ 1286, à Worksop, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Sutton, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Isabella Patrick, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Somery en 1302, à Warsop, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé environ février 1338, à Dudley Castle, Dudley, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Dudley, West Midlands, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat 1302 in Warsop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Margaret Somery geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. ja.lifeSummary: John Sutton Iが約1286年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアウオルクソップで生まれた時，父Richard de Suttonは21歳，母Isabella Patrickは27歳であった。1302年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアワーソップでMargaret Someryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は約1338年2月にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアダドリーダドリー カストルで53歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウエスト ミドランズダドリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Sutton I(이)가 대략 1286년 영국 노틴그함실르 워크소프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Sutton(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella Patrick(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1302년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 워소프에서 Margaret Somery(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1338년 2월 영국 워스스터실르 드들리 드들리 카스틀에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 미들랜드즈 드들리에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Margaret Somery em 1302, em Warsop, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Sutton I родился около 1286 г. в местности ворксоп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Sutton было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Isabella Patrick, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Somery в 1302 г. в местности варсоп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер около февраль 1338 г. в местности дудли кастл, дудли, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности дудли, вест мидландс, Англия, Великобритания.
